acts.full_name: Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act
acts.summary: Establishes the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples as B.C.'s framework for reconciliation. Requires alignment of provincial laws with the UN Declaration, a five-year cross-government action plan, annual reporting to the legislature, and enables agreements with Indigenous governing bodies, including joint decision-making.

sections.title: Alignment of laws, action plan and annual reporting
sections.description: Requires government to bring provincial laws into alignment with the UN Declaration, develop and implement a five-year action plan in consultation and cooperation with Indigenous Peoples, and report annually to the legislature on progress.

services.name: Track progress on Declaration Act implementation
services.ms_name: Implement the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act

services.name: Lead cross-government implementation of the Declaration Act

sections.title: Agreements and joint decision-making
sections.description: Allows the Province flexibility to enter into agreements with a broader range of Indigenous governing bodies and to exercise statutory decision-making authority together with them.

services.name: Enter into a shared decision-making agreement with the Province
services.ms_name: Implement the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act
